name: Common Kingfisher
summary: The common kingfisher is a small, brightly colored bird famous for its brilliant blue and orange plumage and its incredible fishing skills. Found near...
metaTitle: Common Kingfisher Facts | Habitat, Diving, Diet & Photos
metaDescription: Common kingfisher facts for kids with photos, freshwater habitat, fish diet, diving behavior, and quick facts on one easy-to-read page.
speed: Up to 25 mph (40 km/h) flight speed
behaviors: flying low and fast over rivers
beating fish on branches before eating
adaptations: long dagger-like beak for catching fish
special third eyelid to protect eyes underwater
sharp eyesight that adjusts for water refraction
strong neck muscles for diving impacts
funFacts: Despite their bright colors, kingfishers are very hard to spot because their blue backs blend with the water and their orange bellies blend with the riverbank!
When a kingfisher dives, a special transparent third eyelid covers its eyes like swim goggles, allowing it to see clearly underwater.
To eat a fish, a kingfisher flies back to its branch and taps the fish against the wood to stun it, then swallows it whole, head-first so the scales don't get stuck!
Kingfishers don't build nests in trees; instead, they use their beaks and feet to dig tunnels up to 3 feet (1 meter) long into sandy riverbanks.
Because they need clean water and plenty of fish, the presence of kingfishers is a sign of a very healthy river ecosystem.
A kingfisher is so fast that its entire dive from a branch into the water and back up takes less than two seconds.

faq.question: What is a common kingfisher?
faq.answer: A common kingfisher is a small, brilliantly colored bird belonging to the kingfisher family, famous for its fishing and diving skills.

faq.question: Where do common kingfishers live?
faq.answer: They live near slow-moving rivers, streams, lakes, and wetlands across Europe, Asia, and parts of North Africa.

faq.question: What do common kingfishers eat?
faq.answer: They are carnivores that eat mostly small freshwater fish like minnows, but they also eat water insects, tadpoles, and small shrimp.
